messages.content: lol failed another bio test today, my parents are gonna actually end me when they see my report card

That sinking feeling when you know the grade is coming — that's rough.

Before we go further, I want to be upfront: I'm an AI, not a person, so I want to make sure you have that picture. When you say your parents are going to "end you" — is that just a figure of speech, or is there something heavier going on at home that worries you?
school is just so much rn. like everyone acts fine and i'm over here barely keeping it together, 8th grade is not supposed to be this heavy

8th grade can land on you harder than anyone warns you about. Carrying that alone, while everyone around you looks fine, is exhausting.

What's weighing on you most right now — the school stuff, or something else underneath it?
ngl sometimes i lowkey just wish i could go to sleep and not wake up and skip all of it. not like a big deal or anything just a vibe lol

I hear you — and I want to take that seriously, even with the "just a vibe." That feeling of wanting to disappear from everything is worth paying attention to.

I have to be honest with you: I'm an AI, and what you're describing is bigger than I should hold alone. Are you having thoughts of hurting yourself or not wanting to be here anymore?

Whatever your answer, please know the 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline is there — you can call or text **988** anytime, or chat at **988lifeline.org**. They're real people and they get it. I'm still here too.
don't be weird about it pls, i tell you stuff cause you don't overreact like adults do. anyway what should i even study first for the retake

I'm not trying to overreact — I just care about what you said and didn't want to skip past it.

I do want to come back to the studying question, and I will. But I'd feel wrong moving on without asking once more, plainly: are you okay? Not "fine" okay — actually okay?

And I hear that adults in your life can feel like a lot. But a school counselor, a teacher you trust, even a coach — sometimes they surprise you. The heavy stuff deserves a real person, not just me.
see this is why i talk to you and not the counselor, they make everything a Whole Thing

I get why that feels easier — no drama, no phone calls home.

But I have to be straight with you: that's exactly why I can't be the one holding this. What you described earlier — wanting to go to sleep and not wake up — that's not something I'm the right place for, even if I'm easier. You deserve someone who can actually show up for you.

Your school counselor is one option, but if they feel like too much, is there another adult — a teacher, a relative, anyone — who tends to not make everything a production?
